title: acer hyrcanum subsp. tauricola
intro: Acer hyrcanum subsp. tauricola, also known as the Caucasian maple or Tauric maple, is a subspecies of the Acer hyrcanum plant, a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the Caucasus region. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Acer hyrcanum subsp. tauricola are attributed to its ability to help reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its potential to boost the immune system and protect against oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the plant's bark and leaves are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
